body.title: CLOSED: Wire external doc fetch into librarian research pipeline
body.result: sources.py curated index + ResearchEngine.fetch_fn, triggers at depth>=0.6, non-blocking on failure (525249b)
body.description:
librarian/research.py _research_deep() has an explicit stub: `sources=[]  # populated when external search is wired`. The method currently synthesizes an answer from the LLM alone with no external grounding. Context 7 MCP is what general CC users lean on for live doc lookup — our librarian IS our equivalent and this stub is the missing piece.

Implement external fetch for _research_deep: (1) add a curated source index (librarian/sources.py) mapping topic patterns to authoritative doc URLs — Anthropic API docs, psycopg2, Python stdlib, ADC README, TheIgors palace index; (2) in _research_deep, match the query against the source index, fetch relevant pages using urllib (already used in research.py for Ollama), extract relevant sections; (3) feed fetched content into the LLM synthesis prompt; (4) populate sources= with the URLs actually used. Non-blocking: if fetch fails, log and continue with empty sources rather than raising.

**Affected files:** agent_datacenter/devices/librarian/research.py (_research_deep implementation); agent_datacenter/devices/librarian/sources.py (new — curated source index)
**Design rules:** no-theigors-imports (ADC must be portable); non-blocking on fetch failure; curated source list first — not open web search
**Scope boundary:** In: curated doc source fetch, sources= populated, synthesis grounded. Out: search engine API integration, full web crawl, vector embedding of fetched content.
**Test plan:** research(query='psycopg2 execute', depth='deep') returns ResearchResult with non-empty sources list. research() with unreachable URL logs error and returns result (not raises). Curated source index has at least Anthropic docs + psycopg2 + Python docs entries.
